jokes.text: A: We are in an area very high pressure or B: God plays bowling.
jokes.setup: Kent asks for a scientific explanation of the severe storm
jokes.punchline: Frink offers a silly 'God bowling' theory alongside actual meteorology
jokes.explanation: Classic Frink - mixing pseudo-science with childlike logic. The A/B structure is crisp and the bowling metaphor is endearingly absurd.

jokes.text: With the balls, bowling, shoe rental, very bad pizza cheese served in square shares.
jokes.setup: Frink's bowling metaphor for God causing storms
jokes.punchline: He elaborates with mundane bowling alley details like bad pizza
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation of the bowling metaphor but loses steam with the rambling delivery and poor translation quality affecting clarity.

jokes.text: Kent Sorry, I sold all my weather jokes to Jay Mohr.
jokes.setup: Kent asks Krusty for his opinion on the bad weather
jokes.punchline: Krusty references selling jokes to a real comedian
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about the comedy industry. Perfect for Krusty's character as a washed-up entertainer, and the Jay Mohr reference is specific enough to be funny.

jokes.text: But I can make sounds funny in your microphone.
jokes.setup: Krusty has no weather material to offer
jokes.punchline: He offers to make weird sounds instead
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Krusty's desperation, but the joke is fairly predictable and not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Hot Wheels to the rescue! The water comes from here pass line few turns S tight, the looping of death, and exit through lunge for mail.
jokes.setup: Marge asks Homer to do something about the roof leak
jokes.punchline: Homer describes fixing the leak like a Hot Wheels track with dramatic racing terminology
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - treating a serious home repair like a toy. The 'looping of death' is a great escalation and very quotable.

jokes.text: when you can not keep the roof over your family you're really a father.
jokes.setup: Marge is criticizing Homer's failure as a provider
jokes.punchline: The obvious typo/mistranslation where she calls him 'really a father' instead of 'not really a father'
jokes.explanation: This appears to be a translation error that accidentally created irony. Not intentional comedy, so scores low on craft.

jokes.text: The teddy bear had survived three points of safety. But he did not survive you!
jokes.setup: Lisa's belongings are getting ruined by the roof leak
jokes.punchline: Her teddy bear survived safety tests but not Homer's incompetence
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke - the teddy bear can survive factory safety tests but not Homer. Nice structure and character-appropriate outrage from Lisa.

jokes.text: Oh, it's raining outside. It also rains inside!
jokes.setup: Homer is leaving for Moe's to escape the family situation
jokes.punchline: He notices it's raining both outside and inside due to the broken roof
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective observational humor. Homer's matter-of-fact delivery of an absurd situation works well.

jokes.text: So what does Stephen Hawkins here? I live here now. I am the new owner the 'Little Caesar's' down the street. Pizza, pizza. Pizza, pizza.
jokes.setup: Homer is surprised to see Stephen Hawking at Lenny's surprise party
jokes.punchline: The world's most famous physicist now owns a Little Caesar's and his voice synthesizer is stuck on the slogan
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant absurdist humor. The contrast between Hawking's genius and owning a chain pizza place, plus the stuck synthesizer, is inspired comedy writing.

jokes.text: Sorry, this button jammed.
jokes.setup: Hawking's synthesizer kept saying 'Pizza, pizza'
jokes.punchline: He explains it's just a technical malfunction
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to the Pizza Pizza gag. Simple explanation that maintains the absurdity while being perfectly logical.

jokes.text: Surprise? What a surprise?
jokes.setup: The guys say Homer would ruin Lenny's surprise if told in advance
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately proves their point by asking what surprise
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - instantly proving he can't keep secrets. Great character integration and timing.

jokes.text: I cook a cake in the shape of bar stool favorite Lenny and you destroy.
jokes.setup: Moe is upset that Homer ruined Lenny's surprise party
jokes.punchline: The surprise was a cake shaped like Lenny's favorite bar stool
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd and specific to Moe's character. A cake shaped like someone's favorite bar stool is perfectly ridiculous.

jokes.text: Who wants frosting ass? I do not thank you the regime of Dr. Atkins.
jokes.setup: Moe offers pieces of the destroyed bar stool cake
jokes.punchline: Someone specifically doesn't want the 'ass' frosting because they're on Atkins diet
jokes.explanation: Decent gross-out humor combined with a topical diet reference. Works but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: Finally, a bar for men who love drink, and watch sexy girls.
jokes.setup: Homer finds a new establishment
jokes.punchline: He states the obvious appeal of what's clearly a strip club/Hooters-style restaurant
jokes.explanation: Pretty obvious joke about Homer's simple male desires. Not particularly clever or surprising.

jokes.text: You do not see the sign, perverse? It does not look at the enormous chests of our waitresses
jokes.setup: Homer is at what's obviously a Hooters-style restaurant
jokes.punchline: The waitress hypocritically tells him not to look while working at a place specifically designed for that
jokes.explanation: Standard irony about the contradiction in breastaurant business model. Competent but predictable.

jokes.text: I have not seen all cheese since I left my Billy Joel albums in the sun.
jokes.setup: Ray comments on Homer's large plate of nachos
jokes.punchline: He compares the melted cheese to his warped Billy Joel albums
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific and absurd comparison. The Billy Joel reference is random enough to be funny and the image of warped albums is vivid.

jokes.text: Any played by the jukebox. Ok 'Let me ca again Once is never enough...' The next song. Yes, that's what I meant. - Yes, absolutely.
jokes.setup: Homer and Ray need to pick a favorite song
jokes.punchline: They both immediately reject the first song that plays and pretend they meant the next one
jokes.explanation: Good awkward comedy about two guys trying to bond over music and immediately failing. The mutual face-saving is relatable.

jokes.text: You're right margin. I should look for a road in scenes open to poets, where yoga retreats.
jokes.setup: Marge criticizes Homer for finding a roofer at a bar
jokes.punchline: Homer sarcastically suggests looking for blue-collar workers at pretentious artsy places
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic comeback from Homer. The contrast between blue-collar roofers and yoga retreats is well-drawn.

jokes.text: Tape me five. I have values. I will tape five for nothing.
jokes.setup: Homer asks Ray to give him five
jokes.punchline: Homer mishears/says 'tape me five' instead of 'give me five'
jokes.explanation: Seems like a translation error that created accidental wordplay. Not particularly clever as intended comedy.

jokes.text: then we will make emmenera Bart C-I-R-C-O-N-C-I-R.
jokes.setup: Marge is spelling out words to hide plans from the kids
jokes.punchline: She's spelling 'circumcision' but gets it wrong and gives up
jokes.explanation: Good parental awkwardness humor. Marge trying to hide an embarrassing topic from the kids but failing at spelling is relatable.

jokes.text: One day this bowl is overflowing, and I will be there.
jokes.setup: Ray asks if Homer checked the roof when he bought the house
jokes.punchline: Homer says he bought it for the 'view' of a toilet bowl
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - treating a toilet overflow as a scenic view. Absurd but delivered with complete sincerity.

jokes.text: Keep my dog? Until when? Not too long ago, until they die.
jokes.setup: Bart asks if Grampa can keep Santa's Little Helper
jokes.punchline: Grampa matter-of-factly says until the dog dies, implying he'll outlive it
jokes.explanation: Classic dark Grampa humor. The casual acceptance of mortality is both morbid and funny, perfectly in character.

jokes.text: One, two, three, four. I declare war nails! That's why I'm embarked on the roof.
jokes.setup: Homer is working on the roof
jokes.punchline: He declares war on nails like it's a military operation
jokes.explanation: Decent Homer absurdity - treating home repair as warfare. The 'embarked' phrasing adds to the military metaphor.

jokes.text: Ben if I had been, it would had children greatest.
jokes.setup: Marge says Homer isn't married to Ray
jokes.punchline: Homer claims he and Ray would have had better children than Homer and Marge
jokes.explanation: Good Homer absurdity about hypothetical marriage to Ray producing superior offspring.

jokes.text: You know it's bad. It was also from that dog long as I can remember. What dog? - Who are you?
jokes.setup: Grampa has been caring for Santa's Little Helper
jokes.punchline: He's already forgotten both the dog and Bart due to his dementia
jokes.explanation: Dark but perfect Grampa moment. His rapid deterioration from bonding with the dog to forgetting everything is both sad and funny.

jokes.text: How do you think I was going react when you painted it? More or less this way.
jokes.setup: Homer shows Marge his portrait of Ray
jokes.punchline: Marge's deadpan reaction to Homer's expectation of her response
jokes.explanation: Good Marge deadpan moment acknowledging Homer should have predicted her negative reaction.

jokes.text: How do you think I was going react when you painted it?
jokes.setup: Homer shows Marge a portrait he painted of Ray
jokes.punchline: Marge's exasperated reaction to Homer's terrible painting
jokes.explanation: Standard visual gag setup - Homer's bad art skills. Predictable but functional character moment.

jokes.text: His truck will turn at the intersection. Just. now! Now! Now! Now.
jokes.setup: Homer insists Ray is coming to dinner
jokes.punchline: He desperately keeps predicting Ray's arrival at specific moments
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Homer's delusion and desperation. The repeated 'now!' builds uncomfortable tension.

jokes.text: My heart, your friend Ray is in your head.
jokes.setup: Homer is insisting Ray exists and will come
jokes.punchline: Marge gently tells him Ray is imaginary
jokes.explanation: Plot revelation moment. Marge's gentle delivery works for her character but the revelation itself isn't particularly funny.

jokes.text: Ray is a Magini anagram of 'imaginary'. My subconscious is a genius.
jokes.setup: Dr. Hibbert explains that Ray Magini is imaginary
jokes.punchline: The name is an anagram of 'imaginary' and Homer is proud of his subconscious
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that retroactively makes the whole episode cleverer. Homer being proud of his subconscious is perfect character work.

jokes.text: My subconscious is a genius.
jokes.setup: Homer learns his made-up name was an anagram
jokes.punchline: Homer takes credit for his subconscious cleverness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer response - taking credit for accidental cleverness with characteristic ego.

jokes.text: It was a rubber covered with Vaseline.
jokes.setup: Homer asks for something that doesn't taste so good
jokes.punchline: Doctor reveals the 'medical equipment' Homer was chewing
jokes.explanation: Standard gross-out humor about Homer accidentally consuming medical equipment.

jokes.text: Real. Not real. Real. Sorry, but recent Homer Historical evidence shows Robin Hood that has not existed.
jokes.setup: Dr. Hibbert is helping Homer distinguish real from imaginary
jokes.punchline: He casually debunks Robin Hood's existence as a historical aside
jokes.explanation: Good intellectual humor that fits Dr. Hibbert's character. The casual way he drops historical facts is amusing.

jokes.text: I filed a tear in the texture of space-time combined with pieces of metal suspension has created a black hole. He intermediary between Homer and Bart, creating a gravitational lens which has absorbed light reflected by Ray.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Bart couldn't see Ray at the hardware store
jokes.punchline: Frink's overly complex pseudo-scientific explanation involving black holes and gravitational lensing
jokes.explanation: Classic Frink pseudo-science using real physics concepts absurdly to explain plot holes.

jokes.text: I filed a tear in the texture of space-time combined with pieces of metal suspension has created a black hole.
jokes.setup: Everyone needs to explain why Bart saw Homer talking to air
jokes.punchline: Frink offers an impossibly complex scientific explanation involving black holes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frink - using incomprehensible pseudoscience to explain simple things. The gravitational lens explanation is wonderfully overcomplicated.

jokes.text: It's easy, I'm an entrepreneur. It's true, you are all crooks. We are all crooks, it's true.
jokes.setup: Ray needs to explain why he disappeared after starting the roof repair
jokes.punchline: He admits all contractors are crooks and everyone agrees
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about contractor stereotypes. The casual acceptance from everyone makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Monday, 21h, on CBS. They say they love All this guy, but I am not freelance.
jokes.setup: Ray mentions watching something on TV
jokes.punchline: He's promoting The Simpsons' own timeslot and network (this is likely a promotional bit)
jokes.explanation: This appears to be a promotional segment that breaks the fourth wall awkwardly. Not really comedy, more like advertising.

jokes.text: Monday at 21h. On CBS. What time? 21h. And if I want to look, what day? Monday. On Monday, 21h. And it's on the radio? No, it's on television.
jokes.setup: Ray is trying to tell Homer about a TV show
jokes.punchline: Homer keeps asking the same questions over and over despite getting clear answers
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer stupidity but goes on too long. The repetition becomes tedious rather than funny. Feels like padding for the promotional content.
